facts.text: Forest Hills got its name when the Cord Meyer Development Company bought 660 acres in 1906 and renamed the area after Forest Park.
Austin Street has long been Queens’ premier shopping and dining strip, home to Eddie’s Sweet Shop founded in 1925 and modern cafés.
The Forest Hills Tennis Club was featured in the 2001 film The Royal Tenenbaums, where Luke Wilson’s character plays a match.
The Long Island Rail Road opened a Forest Hills station in 1906, complete with a brick courtyard and clock tower that matched the nearby Forest Hills Gardens.
From 1915 to 1978, the Forest Hills arena hosted Open Tennis Championships and legendary acts like the Beatles, Simon & Garfunkel, and Bob Dylan.
Luke’s Episcopal Church and the Forest Hills Post Office are both listed on the National Register of Historic Places.
